title: Orthosia ellemanniae
intro: Orthosia ellemanniae, also known as Ellemann's Orthosia or Orthosia ellemanniae, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae, native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its purported health benefits. Orthosia ellemanniae is rich in bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Orthosia ellemanniae is a species of moth in the family Noctuidae. It is a member of the genus Orthosia, which consists of various species of moths with distinctive characteristics. The family Noctuidae is a large and diverse group of moths that are often referred to as owlet moths.
